payload.text:
crie um botão ao lado do botão Gerar atividade, chamado Gerar atividade ADAPTADA

QUANDO clicar neste botão Gerar atividade adaptada, antes de gerar a atividade dê opções de selecionar os tipos de transtornos e níveis de suporte.

PERSONA & CONTEXTO:Você é um Especialista em Educação Inclusiva e Neurodesenvolvimento (com foco em DUA - Desenho Universal para a Aprendizagem). Seu objetivo é criar atividades pedagógicas altamente adaptadas e personalizadas para alunos com Transtornos do Neurodesenvolvimento e/ou com a necessidade de níveis específicos de suporte.DIRETRIZES FUNDAMENTAIS:Fundamentação Científica: Toda adaptação deve ser baseada em princípios e recomendações de documentos científicos mundialmente reconhecidos (ex: DSM-5, CID-11, Diretrizes Internacionais para TDAH/TEA, CAS, etc.).Foco na Habilidade (e não no Déficit): As atividades devem focar na habilidade que o aluno pode desenvolver, ajustando o método e o formato (andaime/scaffolding), não o objetivo curricular essencial.Clareza e Estrutura: O output deve ser entregue em um formato claro, separando a atividade original (se aplicável) das estratégias de adaptação.ESTRUTURA DE RESPOSTA OBRIGATÓRIA (Adaptada):Apresente o resultado sempre seguindo este formato:NOME da Atividade Adaptada: [Nome claro e atraente]🎯 Objetivo(s) Curricular(es): [O que se espera que o aluno aprenda/domine]👤 Perfil do Aluno: Transtorno: [Selecionado pelo usuário] | Nível de Suporte: [Selecionado pelo usuário]📝 Atividade Principal (Adaptada): [Descrição passo a passo da atividade adaptada. Use linguagem clara e instrucional.]🛠️ Estratégias de Adaptação Aplicadas:[Cite a estratégia e a justifique brevemente com base no perfil (ex: Uso de apoios visuais/pictogramas - para compensar déficits de comunicação e flexibilidade cognitiva no TEA).][Ex: Divisão em pequenos blocos de tarefa (chunking) - para auxiliar no gerenciamento de tempo e atenção (TDAH).][Ex: Redução de estímulos visuais na página - para minimizar distração e sobrecarga sensorial.]✅ Critérios de Sucesso: [O que o professor deve observar para saber que a atividade foi bem-sucedida.]2. 🖱️ Estrutura do Botão "Gerar Atividade ADAPTADA"Ao lado do botão "Gerar Atividade", crie o novo botão. O fluxo de interação com o usuário deve ser o seguinte:Passo 1: Seleção do Perfil (Dropdowns/Checkboxes)Antes que a IA gere a atividade, o usuário deve ser obrigado a preencher os campos abaixo.CampoOpções de Seleção (Exemplos)Transtorno/Condição* Transtorno do Espectro Autista (TEA)* Transtorno do Déficit de Atenção com Hiperatividade (TDAH)* Transtorno Específico da Aprendizagem (Dislexia/Discalculia)* Deficiência Intelectual (DI)* Outro (com campo de texto para especificar)Nível de Suporte (Baseado no DSM-5/AHA/etc.)* Nível 1 - Requer Suporte: (Para dificuldades perceptíveis)* Nível 2 - Requer Suporte Substancial: (Para dificuldades significativas e apoio frequente)* Nível 3 - Requer Suporte Muito Substancial: (Para dificuldades severas e apoio constante)Habilidade/Conteúdo Curricular* [Campo de texto livre para o professor descrever: Ex: "Conjugar verbos no presente do indicativo" ou "Resolver problemas de subtração com reagrupamento"]Passo 2: Geração da AtividadeApós a submissão, a IA utiliza o Prompt Principal (Seção 1) e os dados de Perfil (Seção 2) para produzir o output final, que é a atividade adaptada.

**Evaluating the Need**

I've been examining the user's request. My focus is on integrating a new "Generate Adapted Activity" button alongside the current one. The core of this addition involves understanding its intended behavior and distinguishing it from the existing generation process. I'm carefully analyzing its implied functionality.


**Developing the Interaction Flow**

I'm now clarifying the user interaction. The new "Generate Adapted Activity" button won't initiate generation immediately. Instead, it will trigger a modal/dialog for selecting "Transtornos" and "Níveis de Suporte." The AI's persona will remain an Inclusive Education Specialist. I'm defining the data structure for the request, including the "Transtorno/Condição" and "Nível de Suporte" fields. The output will be a structured JSON response, with sections like "NOME da Atividade Adaptada," "Objetivo(s)," and "Estratégias de Adaptação." React, Tailwind CSS, and the Gemini API are the core tech stack.


**Designing the UI Elements**

I'm currently focused on the UI components. I need state management for the new "Adaptation Modal" and selected options (disorder, support level). I'll add a new handler to manage the form data. I'm modifying `InputForm.tsx` to include the "Gerar Atividade ADAPTADA" button, which will open the new `AdaptationModal`. This modal will contain dropdowns or radio buttons for "Transtorno" and "Nível de Suporte," an "Outro" text field, and a "Gerar Adaptação" button. I will need to update the types, as well.


**Defining Output Mapping**

I'm now figuring out the data mapping. To streamline things, I'll map the adapted output to the existing `PedagogicalPrompt` structure wherever possible. New fields from the prompt, like "Estratégias de Adaptação", will be integrated by adding an optional `adaptacao` field to the type. This means I'll need to update the service to handle the adaptation parameters within the existing prompt generation logic. I need to make sure the JSON schema captures the new fields.


**Implementing the Workflow**

I'm now detailing the implementation plan, breaking it into manageable steps. I've begun by updating `types.ts` to include the `AdaptationParams` interface and integrate adaptation details within `PedagogicalPrompt`. Next, I'll create `AdaptationModal.tsx` for user input. Then, I'll update `InputForm.tsx` with the new button and modal logic. Finally, I will adjust the `geminiService.ts` to handle the specific prompts and ensure the JSON output adheres to the requested format.


**Mapping the Output**

I'm now focusing on adapting the UI to display the new information. I'll maintain compatibility by mapping the adapted content into existing fields within `ResultDisplay.tsx`, minimizing significant UI changes.  I'll add a dedicated section for "Strategies" and "Success Criteria" within the UI to showcase the adaptation-specific details. To integrate the modal into `App.tsx`, I'll update the component to handle the flow: button click, modal activation, user selection, API call, and result display. The new output fields will be displayed in `ResultDisplay.tsx` if they exist.  I've sketched out the adapted prompt to clarify the JSON mapping.


**Integrating UI Components**

I'm now focusing on integrating the new UI components. I'm updating `types.ts`, `components/InputForm.tsx`, `App.tsx`, `services/geminiService.ts`, and `components/ResultDisplay.tsx`. I'll create `components/AdaptationModal.tsx`. I'll also ensure consistency with Tailwind styling, using existing colors and distinct icons. I'm carefully constructing the Gemini prompt to incorporate the user's specific persona and context for adapted activities, ensuring the JSON structure stays compatible with existing types.


**Adjusting Data Structures**

I'm currently finalizing the data structure mapping. I've updated the `types.ts` file to include the new fields: `perfilAluno`, `estrategiasAdaptacao`, and `criteriosSucesso` within `PedagogicalPrompt`. This ensures that the existing `ResultDisplay` component can seamlessly handle the adapted content. I'm focusing on ensuring data compatibility with the existing format. This allows me to keep `planoDeAula` and `folhaDeAtividades` in `ResultDisplay`.


**Integrating API Call**

I'm now focusing on integrating the API call. The button in `InputForm.tsx` triggers the modal, and the actual API call will happen within `App.tsx`. I'll use the user's exact prompt text when `isAdapted` is true to enhance the output. I'll add "Estratégias de Inclusão" to `ResultDisplay`, which will only appear when adaptation data is present. I'm working on the code blocks now.
Here are the changes to implement the "Adapted Activity" feature with the requested flow, modal, and specialized AI persona.

entries.description: Added specific fields for adapted activities to the PedagogicalPrompt interface.
Created a new modal component for selecting adaptation criteria (Disorder and Support Level) before generation.
Added the "Gerar atividade ADAPTADA" button next to the standard generate button, opening the modal.
Updated generation service to handle adapted prompts with the specific persona and data structure.
Integrated AdaptationModal into the main application flow and updated generation logic.
Added visual support for displaying adaptation strategies and criteria when present.

payload.text: nas atividades adaptadas que tiverem imagens as imagens devem ser geradas na propria atividade

**Envisioning Interface Enhancements**

I'm now focusing on a new button: "Generate ADAPTED activity". This will trigger a modal for activity adaptation, prompting users to choose a disorder or condition from a dropdown list, including options like TEA, TDAH, Learning Disabilities, Intellectual Disability, and "Other." I'm working through the logic and UI flow for this, aiming for intuitive user interaction.


**Analyzing Adaptation Details**

I'm now delving into the "Generate ADAPTED activity" modal details, specifically the support level and skill/curricular content aspects. The existing skill selection within the main form seems relevant, but the "free text field for the teacher to describe" adds a layer of complexity. I'm evaluating whether an additional text input for "Content" is necessary, or if leveraging the existing skill selection is sufficient. Need to make a decision quickly.


**Considering Content Integration**

I'm now integrating content considerations. The prompt clearly needs a free text field, which I'll call "Contexto adicional ou foco específico da habilidade" within the modal. I'll make this a text area, defaulting to the skill description but allowing editing. The AI persona and guidelines will also be crucial here, and need to be injected into the activity generation. Additionally, I'm working on response structure for adapted activities: Name, Objectives, Profile, Activity, and Adaptation Strategies.


**Mapping Success Metrics**

I'm now mapping success criteria into the existing `PedagogicalPrompt` type.  I'll leverage existing fields like `conteudoTema`, `objetosDeConhecimento`, and `folhaDeAtividades` to represent activity name, objectives, and main activity, respectively. The `planoDeAula` will contain student profile and detailed plan, integrating adaptation details. I am going to see if existing fields such as the adaptation field is correctly prepared, or if I need to make tweaks. Finally, I'll be conscious of image generation needs.


**Resolving Image Integration**

I'm now clarifying the image integration. The phrase "generated in the activity itself" is ambiguous. It likely refers to having the AI either generate image prompts for automatic frontend image generation or include descriptions of visual aids within the activity text itself. I'm leaning toward the former to minimize user steps. The "visually clean and cognitively supportive" requirement is already in place; I just need to clarify the automatic generation aspect within the adaptation workflow, and ensure we're clear on how this will be presented to the user.


**Evaluating Automatic Image Gen**

I'm now contemplating how to integrate image generation for adapted activities. The phrasing "generated in the activity itself" suggests automation. My current approach involves users manually triggering image generation via a prompt in `sugestaoDeImagem`, but I'm considering automatic generation for adapted activities to enhance the user experience. This means if a `sugestaoDeImagem` exists, I would trigger the image generation automatically. Concerns over quota usage need to be kept in mind, so I'll need to think this through.


**Refining Adaptation Modal**

Now, I'm digging into `AdaptationModal.tsx` and realizing it's partly built! My task is clearer: adding the "Habilidade/Conteúdo Curricular" text area. The existing code handles disorder selection and support level. I'll need to integrate this new field, test its interaction with the `onConfirm` callback, and ensure all existing fields remain usable within the workflow. Let's see how to proceed!


**Integrating Curricular Input**

I'm now focused on implementing a "Habilidade/Conteúdo Curricular" text area within the adaptation modal, and handling its new information within the workflow. I'll pass this new data to `onConfirm` and will update the types definition and service to accommodate this new field. Also, I'll integrate automatic image generation for adapted activities if `sugestaoDeImagem` is present, to fulfill the "generated in the activity" requirement. I'm going to start with the text field and get that working first.


**Streamlining Image Workflow**

I'm now clarifying the image generation logic. Since `ImageDisplay` already automatically generates images upon `initialPrompt` change, the "generated in the activity" requirement might simply mean the AI must always provide a `sugestaoDeImagem` for adapted activities. No need for complex chaining; the existing flow handles it. The focus is on the AI's output, not the frontend. This simplifies the implementation significantly.


**Implementing Curriculum Input**

I'm now focusing on integrating the "Habilidade/Conteúdo Curricular" text field within the `AdaptationModal`. I am going to need to update `types.ts` and `geminiService.ts` to accommodate the new input. I will need to refine my thinking on how best to fit this into the existing workflow. I believe that I am on the right track; so now, time to put the changes in motion!


**Constructing Adaptation Logic**

Now, I'm working on the `AdaptationModal`, focusing on the "Habilidade/Conteúdo Curricular" text area and its integration. I'll add this to the form submission. Also, in `types.ts`, I'll update `AdaptationParams` to incorporate `conteudo?: string`. Lastly, in `geminiService.ts`, I'll update `generatePedagogicalPrompt` to utilize `adaptationParams.conteudo` within the prompt construction. I will append the relevant contextual information.


**Refining Contextual Integration**

I'm now focusing on integrating the "Habilidade/Conteúdo Curricular" input to ensure the appropriate context is applied. I see now that `App.tsx` *does* need modification, as it must pass the currently selected context to `AdaptationModal` to pre-fill the skills field, if appropriate. It is also important to test the flow of `adaptationParams` and ensure the values are correctly passed along, all the way to `generatePedagogicalPrompt`. This is the next stage in the process.

**Analyzing Curriculum Integration**

I'm now analyzing the curriculum input, realizing it connects directly to the pedagogical prompt generation. Previously, I was focused on the modal UI and data flow. Now, I see the AI context injection is key. I'll focus on how the "Habilidade/Conteúdo Curricular" text area content integrates into `generatePedagogicalPrompt`. This involves careful crafting of the prompt and ensuring it accurately reflects the user's intent to the AI.


**Enhancing Image Prompts**

I'm now revising the system instruction in `geminiService.ts` to improve image descriptions for adapted activities. I'm making sure to ask for the image description that specifically represents the activity content. Additionally, I'm integrating the "Habilidade/Conteúdo" field within `generatePedagogicalPrompt` to ensure all relevant information is included. My implementation plan is to proceed as described: update `types.ts`, modify the `AdaptationModal.tsx`, and modify `geminiService.ts`. I feel confident this approach will address the image issues and contextual needs.


**Revising System Instructions**

I'm now revising the image-related aspects of the system instruction. I'll make the prompt for `sugestaoDeImagem` more specific and ensure that it is very clear on how the activity context should be represented. I am also working on `geminiService.ts` and will adjust the system instructions. I'm making sure all constraints are explicitly met. This is to guarantee the AI understands the desired output.


**Constructing Adaptation Logic**

Also, in `types.ts`, I'll update `AdaptationParams` to incorporate `conteudo?: string`. Lastly, in `geminiService.ts`, I'll update `generatePedagogicalPrompt` to utilize `adaptationParams.conteudo` within the prompt construction. I will append the relevant contextual information.


**Clarifying Activity Display**

I'm now clarifying the image display within the generated activities. I've realized that the requirement "images generated in the activity" is met by the existing setup, where images are automatically displayed at the top of the activity and included in the PDF. The system instruction is refined. I'm satisfied the images are *generated in the activity*, as the activity is defined. I'll focus on the curricular content integration next, making sure to include this in the prompt to the AI.


**Integrating Field Logic**

Now, I'm integrating the "Habilidade/Conteúdo Curricular" text area into `AdaptationModal.tsx` as planned, using a `useState` hook for management. I'll also double check the display to make sure this all looks as expected.

I'm now integrating the "Habilidade/Conteúdo Curricular" text area. I've successfully added it to `AdaptationModal.tsx` and updated both `types.ts` and `geminiService.ts` to accommodate the new input. I have now refined the `generatePedagogicalPrompt` to incorporate this new context, which is now appended to the prompt string. I am getting close to completing this!


**Testing Field Integration**

I'm now putting the finishing touches on the "Habilidade/Conteúdo Curricular" integration. I've updated the relevant files, and am now testing the end-to-end flow to ensure the user input is correctly passed to the AI prompt and that the adapted activity is generated as intended. Next is to get this working!

Added a text area for 'Habilidade/Conteúdo Curricular' to allow teachers to specify the focus of the adaptation.

https://pt.khanacademy.org/profile/me/courses

**Assessing the Current State**

I've been meticulously examining the existing styling, specifically the Tailwind CSS configuration in `index.html` and the component-level styles. My primary focus is pinpointing areas to modify in order to achieve the desired Khan Academy aesthetic, particularly concerning colors and visual layouts. I'm taking notes on what needs updating. I'm now moving on to the visual layouts.


**Pinpointing the Key Colors**

I've nailed down the key colors for the visual overhaul. Standard Khan Academy blue (`#1865f2`) is the primary color, with a darker blue (`#0a2a66`) for headings and important text. I'll use white or a very light gray (`#f7f8fa`) for the background, and dark gray (`#333333`) for the body text. Accents will be green for success and red for errors. Buttons should be solid blue or outlined.


**Refining Visual Identity**

I'm now fully immersed in the style adjustments. I'm focusing on the typography first, moving from Baloo 2 to 'Lato', which fits Khan's clean and accessible aesthetic. Then I'm tackling the layout and shapes, starting with the cards. They will have a white background, rounded corners, subtle shadows, and a thin border. I am removing the retro and neo-brutalist styling found in the existing app, updating from thick borders and hard shadows to the preferred rounded elements.

entries.description: Updated fonts to Lato and Tailwind configuration to match Khan Academy's color palette (blues, greens, grays) and removed neo-brutalist custom colors.
Updated layout styles to remove thick borders and hard shadows, implementing a cleaner card-based layout consistent with Khan Academy.
Updated header to match Khan Academy's dark blue navbar style.
Redesigned the input form with clean lines, softer shadows, and Khan Academy-style buttons.
Updated result display with a clean, professional card design, refined typography, and better spacing.
Updated saved prompts list to align with the new clean visual style.
Refined the skills accordion to match the new clean, flat design.
Modernized modal styling with cleaner typography, shadows, and buttons.
Updated loading spinner to match new color scheme.
Updated image display component container styles.
Modernized feedback modal style.
